content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662         322-5660         322-5901         322-6441         322-6444

April 5, 1983


Assemblyman Burt Margolin
State Capitol, Rm. 6011
Sacramento, CA  95814

Attn: Colleen Beamish

                                        Re: Our Advice No. A-83-091

Dear Assemblyman Margolin:

     I am writing in response to your recent question to
documents examiner Alice Hughes concerning whether or not
reimbursements of expenses, or repayments of loans, by a
campaign committee should be reported as income on a Statement
of Economic Interests. As a general rule, any "payment
received" is considered income under the Political Reform Act,
Government Code Section §2030,1/ and may have to be reported
on a Statement of Economic Interests. Section 2030 provides
some exceptions to this rule (none of which are relevant to your
question). The Commission has established some additional
exceptions for certain limited categories of payments from
campaign committees when reporting those payments would not
serve any purpose under the Act.

     In an Opinion requested by Assemblyman Willie Brown, Jr.,
1 FPPC 67 (1975), the Commission ruled that a candidate does not
need to report on his Statement of Economic Interests the
reimbursement of his campaign expenses by his controlled
committee. The Commission reasoned that, since the candidate
must disclose the reimbursement on his committee's campaign
statement, which he must personally verify, no purpose could be
served by additional disclosure of the reimbursements on his

___________________
     1/ .All statutory references made are to the Government
Code unless otherwise stated.

Assemblyman Burt Margolin
April 5, 1983
Page Two


Statement of Economic Interests. In accordance with this
opinion, and with Section 82030, payments to a public official
from a campaign committee should be disclosed as follows:

     1. Reimbursement of an official's expenses, or direct
payments for the official's expenses,2/ by that official's
controlled committee need not be disclosed on the official's
Statement of Economic Interests.

     2. Repayment of an official's expenses, or direct payment
for an official's expenses, by a committee which is not
controlled by the official must be reported on the official's
Statement of Economic Interests.

     3. Repayment of the principal of a loan made by a public
official from his personal funds to that official's controlled
committee need not be reported on the official's Statement of
Economic Interests. Payment of interest on the loan by the
controlled committee must be reported, however.

     4. Repayment of both principal and interest of a loan made
by a public official to a committee which is not controlled by
the official must be reported on the official's Statement of
Economic Interests.

     If you have any further questions about your Statement of
Economic Interests, please contact Alice Hughes at 916/322-6444.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Barbara A. Milman
                                        Chief, Legal Division

BAM:km

_______________________

     2/ Of course, these expenses must be campaign related or
have a governmental purpose. Elections Code Sections
12400-12407.

sections.conclusion: In accordance with Government Code Section 82030 and prior FPPC Opinion (Assemblyman Willie Brown, Jr., 1 FPPC 67 (1975)), payments to a public official from a campaign committee should be disclosed as follows: (1) Reimbursement of an official's expenses by that official's controlled committee need not be disclosed on the official's Statement of Economic Interests; (2) Repayment of an official's expenses by a committee which is not controlled by the official must be reported on the official's Statement of Economic Interests; (3) Repayment of the principal of a loan made by a public official from personal funds to that official's controlled committee need not be reported, but payment of interest must be reported; (4) Repayment of both principal and interest of a loan made by a public official to a committee which is not controlled by the official must be reported on the official's Statement of Economic Interests.
sections.question_synthetic: Should a public official report reimbursements of campaign expenses or loan repayments from campaign committees as income on their Statement of Economic Interests?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Reimbursements and loan repayments are generally considered income under Government Code Section 82030, but the FPPC created exceptions for controlled committees where disclosure would be redundant with campaign statement reporting. Uncontrolled committees must have all reimbursements and loan repayments reported, except principal repayments from controlled committees are exempt while interest payments are not.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Clear FPPC response letter with well-structured question and conclusion sections. Document contains four-part ruling with specific guidance on controlled vs. non-controlled committees and different treatment of expense reimbursements versus loan repayments.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises Assemblyman Burt Margolin on reporting requirements for campaign committee reimbursements and loan repayments on Statements of Economic Interests, establishing different rules based on whether the committee is controlled by the official and the type of payment involved.
